task_completion.description: Import the aftershock XML data into the SeisComP database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal window showing a command (such as 'scdb') being executed to import '/home/ga/Desktop/aftershock_data.xml' into the database.

task_completion.description: Set the aftershock event type to 'earthquake' using scolv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'scolv' (SeisComP Origin Locator View) GUI window. The aftershock event (ID: us6000m13n) should be selected, and its Event Type field/dropdown must be set to 'earthquake'.

task_completion.description: Set the preferred magnitude type of the aftershock to 'Mw(mB)' using scolv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'scolv' GUI window with the aftershock event (ID: us6000m13n) selected. The preferred magnitude type must be explicitly set to 'Mw(mB)' in the event summary or magnitude tab.

task_completion.description: Export the formatted event bulletin to the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal window showing a command (such as 'scbulletin') being used to export event data and redirecting the output to '/home/ga/Desktop/event_comparison.txt'.

task_completion.description: Verify the exported bulletin contains magnitudes and locations for both the mainshock and aftershock.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command (e.g., 'cat', 'less') or a text editor displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/event_comparison.txt'. The file must clearly show location and magnitude data for both the mainshock (us6000m0xl) and the aftershock (us6000m13n).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the scolv GUI to modify the event type and magnitude.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent actually opened and interacted with the 'scolv' application windows to make changes, rather than bypassing the GUI by manually editing database files or using unsupported scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the bulletin was generated using SeisComP CLI tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the terminal history to ensure a tool like 'scbulletin' was used to generate the text file, rather than the agent manually typing out the expected output in a text editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not hardcode the output file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look out for 'echo' commands or text editor usage that manually pastes the event IDs, magnitudes, and locations into '/home/ga/Desktop/event_comparison.txt' without querying the actual SeisComP database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Mainshock Event ID: us6000m0xl. Aftershock Event ID: us6000m13n (synthetic 6.2 mag event). Aftershock XML path: /home/ga/Desktop/aftershock_data.xml. Required GUI app: scolv. Required CLI tools: scdb (for import) and scbulletin (for export). Output file: /home/ga/Desktop/event_comparison.txt.
